title: Minimal coded state census (read-only)
intent: Add a NEW read-only census script (scripts/state_census.py) and its relationship test (tests/test_state_census.py) that classifies every open position across six axes (position truth / redeem truth / command truth / fill truth / quote-or-exit truth / identity truth) using ONLY existing canonical DB read surfaces. Empty settlement command queue is reported as data_unavailable, never as no_redeem_queued. The census never writes to any production DB.

asserted_invariants.text: scripts/state_census.py opens DB connections in read-only URI mode (file:?mode=ro) and never executes INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE, ALTER, or DROP on any production DB. test_census_read_only asserts this with a sqlite3 trace hook or by attempting and catching a write that the read-only connection refuses.

When the settlement_commands table contains zero rows for a position's condition_id, the census classifies redeem_truth as 'data_unavailable' and explicitly NOT 'no_redeem_queued'. The two values are distinct in the census output schema and in test_census_data_unavailable_on_empty_settlement_queue.

When a venue_submission_envelope row has condition_id starting with 'legacy:' or question_id == 'legacy-compat', the census flags identity_truth as 'placeholder' and includes the trade_id in an anomalies list. test_census_detects_placeholder_identity asserts this with a fixture-backed envelope row.

When a position has corrected_executable_economics_eligible=True AND fill_authority is NOT FILL_AUTHORITY_VENUE_CONFIRMED_FULL (or whatever T0/T1F-locked authority constants are), the census classifies position_truth as 'review_required' and includes the trade_id in an anomalies list. test_census_detects_corrected_row_without_fill_authority asserts this.

Both new files (scripts/state_census.py and tests/test_state_census.py) carry the file-header provenance block per ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md (Created / Last reused or audited / Authority basis fields). architecture/script_manifest.yaml gets a new row for state_census.py with class=diagnostic_read_only (or equivalent), apply_flag absent, target_db=zeus_trades.db (read-only), required_tests including tests/test_state_census.py. architecture/test_topology.yaml gets a new row for tests/test_state_census.py with created/last_used dates.

round2_grep_verification.scripts_state_census_does_not_exist_yet: ls scripts/state_census.py → 'No such file or directory' at planner Round-2.
round2_grep_verification.tests_state_census_does_not_exist_yet: ls tests/test_state_census.py → 'No such file or directory' at planner Round-2.
round2_grep_verification.settlement_commands_read_helpers_already: src/execution/settlement_commands.py:444 SELECT * FROM settlement_commands ... WHERE state = ? — provides the read shape; T1H imports the read API rather than crafting raw SQL.
round2_grep_verification.portfolio_read_helpers_already_exist: src/state/portfolio.py:1761 def get_open_positions(...) and src/state/portfolio.py:1715 corrected_executable_economics_eligible projection — the census reads these.
round2_grep_verification.venue_command_repo_envelope_reads: src/state/venue_command_repo.py:319 def insert_submission_envelope (write API; the census uses the corresponding read API at the same module — planner did not enumerate exhaustively).

_planner_notes.loc_estimate_basis: scripts/state_census.py (new): ~400 lines — 6 census axes × ~50 lines per classifier + ~50 lines for argv parsing + JSON output + ~50 lines for the read-only connection helper. tests/test_state_census.py (new): ~300 lines — 4 named invariant tests plus fixture setup. architecture/script_manifest.yaml: +12 lines. architecture/test_topology.yaml: +1 line. Total ~700 LOC; this is by far the largest T1 phase but is mostly NEW additive code, not edits to existing surfaces.
_planner_notes.cross_module_edges_basis: 4 imports across module boundaries — src.state.portfolio (Position dataclass + reads), src.execution.settlement_commands (SettlementState enum + reads), src.state.venue_command_repo (envelope reads), src.config (state_path / DB locations). Each is read-only.
_planner_notes.code_provenance_header: Per ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md (Code Provenance section): every new script and test file carries Created/Last-reused-or-audited/Authority-basis header. T1H-PROVENANCE-HEADER-PRESENT invariant enforces this; the critic verifies header presence.
_planner_notes.topology_admission: BLOCKED at planner time — generic profile does not admit scripts/state_census.py for write-intent create. Resolution per LOCK_DECISION Amendment 4. NOTE: scripts/state_census.py creation may match the 'add or change script' digest profile better than the surgical executor work in T1A/T1F — coordinator may try that profile name before declaring STOP_REPLAN.
_planner_notes.k0_k1_classification: READ-ONLY surface; lowest-risk T1 phase. Critic-opus still required (the census produces evidence that gates Tier-2 admission; correctness of read interpretation matters even if the script writes nothing).
coordinator_blockers: T0.3 venue quiescent attestation pending (planner Round-1).
_planner_notes.predecessor_dependency: T1G MUST land first — the census's identity_truth axis classifies envelope persistence; if T1G found NEEDS_FIX persistence gaps the census results would be misleading. T1A/T1F/T1BD/T1C/T1E provide the invariants that the census's anomaly-detection logic encodes.
_planner_notes.closeout_drift_resolution: ImportError fallback drift (C-1 LOW: scripts/state_census.py hardcoded duplicate of FILL_AUTHORITY_VENUE_CONFIRMED_FULL='venue_confirmed_full' on src.state.portfolio import failure) was RESOLVED by T2F-CENSUS-FILL-AUTHORITY-FAIL-CLOSED invariant. Post-T2F the census exits non-zero with 'authority constant unavailable; refusing to classify' rather than silently using the duplicate string; tests/test_state_census.py::test_census_fail_closed_on_missing_authority_constant asserts.
